name: Tomato Bread
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Oil-packed sun-dried tomatoes, coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly sauté the garlic and onion in oil until cooled to room temperature.
2. In a large bowl, stir yeast into warm water; let stand until creamy, about 10 minutes.
3. Stir in remaining cup of water, garlic, onion with oil, and sun-dried tomatoes.
4. Mix flour and salt; gradually fold 1 cup at a time into the yeast mixture.
5. Knead dough on a lightly floured surface, adding additional flour as needed until soft, velvety, and slightly moist (8-10 minutes).
6. In a food processor with steel blade or dough blade, mix flour and salt briefly.
7. Add garlic, onion with oil, and sun-dried tomatoes to the processor; pour dissolved yeast and 1 cup water through feed tube quickly as flour absorbs it.
8. Process until dough forms; knead for 30 seconds more.
9. Finish kneading briefly by hand on a floured surface if desired.
10. Shape dough into a ball, place on lightly oiled baking sheet or peel covered with flour, cover with towel, and let rise doubled (45-55 minutes).
11. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). If using a baking stone, preheat 30 minutes before baking; sprinkle with cornmeal just before placing loaf on it.
12. Slash the top of the loaf with a razor blade in three places.
13. Brush with egg white and bake for 10 minutes, spraying with water 3 times.
14. Reduce heat to 375°F (190°C) and continue baking 25-30 minutes longer.
15. Cool completely on rack.
